_meta.purpose: Single source of truth for all visual elements. Every prompt across every scene composes from these fields. Update here, not in individual prompts.
_meta._template_note: Fork this file per project. Strip the placeholders, fill in your specifics, save it alongside your generated assets so every sub-agent and every future session reads the same source of truth.

tech.casting_pipeline_pattern: 28 references per character: A1-A8 studio identity locks (8) + B1-B8 real-world contexts (8) + C1-C6 expressions (6) + D1-D6 lighting variations (6). See HIGGSFIELD_LESSONS_LEARNED.md.

<character_name>.soul_id_v2: <UUID after Soul 2.0 training in higgsfield.ai web UI>
<character_name>.soul_id_cinematic: <UUID after Soul Cinematic training in higgsfield.ai web UI>
<character_name>.face: face description, distinguishing marks, age indicators
<character_name>.energy_reference: <actor name in <film name>> — e.g. 'Jeremy Renner in The Town'
<character_name>.wardrobe_primary: specific items, colors, condition. Avoid logos.
<character_name>.wardrobe_rule: no logos, no brand marks, no text on clothing
<character_name>.casting_pipeline_required: 28 reference images: A1-A8 studio identity locks, B1-B8 real-world contexts (relevant to your character's life), C1-C6 expressions (emotional vocabulary the film needs), D1-D6 lighting variations (the lighting arc of the film)

vehicles.<vehicle_name>: Year, color, make, model, body style, condition, distinguishing details. Include STRICT constraints to prevent model drift (e.g., 'STRICT: single cab long box only — no Chevy, no crew cab, no extended cab.')

locations.<location_name>: Environment description, time-of-day characteristics, atmospheric details, distinctive set dressing

<palette_name>.key_phrase: Compact prose description suitable to drop into a prompt's lighting section
<palette_name>.discipline: Specific rules to enforce — e.g., 'the warm practical must be the ONLY warm light source — no other warm spill on truck body or surroundings'

<panel_id>.url: https://... (Higgsfield CDN URL of the rendered frame)
<panel_id>.use_as: composition reference for any subsequent shot that needs to rhyme with this frame
<panel_id>.note: Description of what makes this frame anchor-worthy

anchor_media_ids._note: Pre-uploaded Higgsfield workspace media IDs from the casting pipeline. Use as composition references via the medias array on cinematic_studio_2_5 / soul_2 generations. Format: [{"value": "<media_id>", "role": "image"}]
anchor_media_ids._per_character: Each character should have their own anchor_media_ids block, populated after the casting pipeline uploads their 28 references. The `media_ids.json` file in their casting_dir contains the full mapping.

global_directives.color_grade_rule: raw documentary realism, naturalistic muted palette, NO teal-orange grade, monochromatic with controlled warm point sources, subtle 35mm film grain
global_directives.negative_prompts_global: no other warm light spill, no extra reflections, no logos, no text on clothing or signs unless specifically requested, no other vehicles in frame, no people standing outside unless specified
global_directives._resolution_caveat: Soul 2.0 silently caps at 1080p (1344x768) regardless of resolution request. Plan accordingly per HIGGSFIELD_LESSONS_LEARNED.md.
